title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: He had said to him, “I will keep him busy while you strike him with a sword from behind.” Thus, Arbad placed his hand on his sword in preparation. As the Prophet  stood and spoke with ‘Amir alone, next to a wall, Arbad grabbed his sword. But each time he wanted to unsheath the sword, his hand would become stiff, and thus he wasn’t able to do it. ‘Amir was keeping the Prophet  busy and looking at Arbad at the same time, whilst Arbad remained stiff, unable to move. The Prophet  turned to Arbad to see what he was doing, and Who kicked the cat? then turned to ‘Amir and said, “O ‘Amir bin al-Tufayl, accept Islam.” ‘Amir replied, “O Muhammad, what would you give me in return for becoming a Muslim?” The Prophet  replied, “You will have what all the Muslims have and you would be expected to do what all the Muslims are expected to do.” ‘Amir said, “Do you agree to give me authority after you, if I was to embrace Islam?” The Prophet  did not want to promise ‘Amir that which might not be fulﬁlled. Hence, he was boldly straightforward with him and said, “Authority is neither for you nor for your people.” ‘Amir attempted to make his demands more plausible, so he said, “I will accept Islam if you give me authority over the Bedouins and you keep authority over the cities.” But the Prophet  did not want to oblige himself with a promise would not fulﬁl.Thus, he said,“No.” Thereupon, ‘Amir became angry, his complexion changed and shouted at the top of his voice, “I shall ﬁll your spaces with men and cavalry and tie a horse to every tree. I will attack you with two thousand horses!” He then left, foaming at the mouth and screaming. The Prophet  looked at him and then raised his gaze to the sky and said, “O Allah, sufﬁce me of Amir’s evil and guide his people.” ‘Amir set off with his companions until he left Madinah head63 Who kicked the cat? ing towards the dwellings of his people, intending to go back and prepare an army to attack Madinah. During the way, he became tired and the signs of exhaustion became apparent on him. He needed a place to rest, and by chance, he was passing by a woman from his people, known as Saluliyyah who used to live in a tent. She was known as a debauched woman. She was condemned by the people and whoever entered her house was accused of debauchery and evil. Since, ‘Amir could not ﬁnd a place to stay, he had no choice but to descend from his horse and sleep in her house. Overnight, he caught a disease in the thyroid gland near his throat which usually afﬂicts camels and due to which they die. He became terriﬁed and confused. He began to feel his tumour and say, “It is the sort of tumour that camels get, and it is death in the house of Saluliyyah!” Meaning, it was neither an honourable death, nor was the place honourable. He wished that he could be killed in a battle, struck by the swords of brave men. Yet, there he was, dying of a disease that usually afﬂicts animals, and in the house of a debauched woman! Woe to this humiliation and disgrace! He screamed to his companions, “Bring me my horse!” When they brought him his horse, he mounted it and took hold of his spear. He began to roam around on his horse, while screaming in pain. He kept feeling his throat with his hands and saying, “It is the sort of tumour that camels get, and it is death in the house of Saluliyyah!” He continued moving about on his horse in this manner until he eventually fell from his horse, dead. Who kicked the cat? His companions left him and went back to their people. When they entered their dwellings, the people came to Arbad and asked him what happened. He said, “Nothing much. Muhammad called us to worship something… I wish I was with him now, so I could shoot him with an arrow and kill him!” SubhanAllah! How audacious! A couple of days after he had said this, he left to sell his camel. Allah struck him and his camel with lightning, which annihilated both of them. Allah revealed with respect to ‘Amir and Arbad, “Allah knows that which every female bears and that which the wombs absorb and that which they grow. And everything with Him is measured. He is the Knower of the Invisible and the Visible, the Great, the High Exalted. Alike of you is he who hides the saying and he who noises it abroad, he who lurks in the night and he who goes freely in the daytime. For him are angels ranged before him and behind him, who guard him by Allah’s command.
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_id: Ia berkata kepadanya, "Aku akan membuatnya sibuk sementara kamu menebasnya dengan pedang dari belakang." Maka Arbad meletakkan tangannya pada pedangnya dalam persiapan. Ketika Nabi berdiri dan berbicara dengan 'Amir berdua saja, di dekat sebuah dinding, Arbad menggenggam pedangnya. Tetapi setiap kali ia ingin menghunus pedang, tangannya menjadi kaku, sehingga ia tidak mampu melakukannya. 'Amir membuat Nabi sibuk sambil memandangi Arbad pada saat yang sama, sementara Arbad tetap kaku, tidak mampu bergerak. Nabi berpaling kepada Arbad untuk melihat apa yang ia lakukan, kemudian berpaling kepada 'Amir dan berkata, "Wahai 'Amir bin ath-Thufail, terimalah Islam." 'Amir menjawab, "Wahai Muhammad, apa yang akan kamu berikan kepadaku sebagai imbalan menjadi Muslim?" Nabi menjawab, "Kamu akan mendapatkan apa yang dimiliki semua Muslim dan kamu diharapkan melakukan apa yang diharapkan dari semua Muslim." 'Amir berkata, "Apakah kamu setuju untuk memberiku otoritas setelahmu, jika aku memeluk Islam?" Nabi tidak ingin menjanjikan 'Amir sesuatu yang mungkin tidak terpenuhi. Oleh karena itu, beliau berterus terang kepadanya dengan tegas dan berkata, "Otoritas bukan untukmu maupun untuk kaummu." 'Amir mencoba membuat tuntutannya lebih masuk akal, sehingga ia berkata, "Aku akan menerima Islam jika kamu memberiku otoritas atas orang-orang Badui dan kamu memegang otoritas atas kota-kota." Tetapi Nabi tidak ingin mewajibkan dirinya dengan janji yang tidak akan ia penuhi. Dengan demikian, beliau berkata, "Tidak." Lalu 'Amir marah, wajahnya berubah dan berteriak sekeras-kerasnya, "Aku akan memenuhi ruang-ruangmu dengan pasukan dan pasukan berkuda dan mengikat seekor kuda di setiap pohon. Aku akan menyerangmu dengan dua ribu kuda!" Ia kemudian pergi, mulutnya berbusa dan berteriak. Nabi memandangnya kemudian mengangkat pandangannya ke langit dan berkata, "Ya Allah, cukupkanlah aku dari kejahatan 'Amir dan tunjukkanlah kaumnya." 'Amir berangkat bersama teman-temannya hingga meninggalkan Madinah menuju perkampungan kaumnya, bermaksud untuk kembali dan menyiapkan pasukan untuk menyerang Madinah. Di perjalanan, ia merasa lelah dan tanda-tanda kelelahan tampak padanya. Ia butuh tempat untuk beristirahat, dan secara kebetulan, ia melewati seorang wanita dari kaumnya yang dikenal sebagai Saluliyyah yang tinggal di sebuah tenda. Ia dikenal sebagai wanita tidak bermoral. Ia dicela oleh orang-orang dan siapapun yang masuk ke rumahnya dituduh berbuat cabul dan jahat. Karena 'Amir tidak dapat menemukan tempat untuk menginap, ia tidak punya pilihan selain turun dari kudanya dan tidur di rumahnya. Semalam suntuk, ia terkena penyakit pada kelenjar tiroid di dekat tenggorokannya yang biasanya menyerang unta dan menyebabkan kematian mereka. Ia merasa takut dan bingung. Ia mulai merasakan benjolan itu dan berkata, "Ini adalah jenis benjolan yang biasa menyerang unta, dan ini adalah kematian di rumah Saluliyyah!" Artinya, bukan kematian yang mulia, dan tempatnya pun tidak mulia. Ia berharap bisa terbunuh dalam pertempuran, ditebas oleh pedang pria-pria pemberani. Namun di sana ia mati karena penyakit yang biasanya menyerang hewan, dan di rumah seorang wanita tidak bermoral! Celaka betapa hina dan memalukan! Ia berteriak kepada teman-temannya, "Bawakan kudaku!" Ketika mereka membawa kudanya, ia naik ke atasnya dan menggenggam tombaknya. Ia mulai berkeliling dengan kudanya, sambil berteriak kesakitan. Ia terus merasakan tenggorokannya dengan tangannya dan berkata, "Ini adalah jenis benjolan yang biasa menyerang unta, dan ini adalah kematian di rumah Saluliyyah!" Ia terus bergerak di atas kudanya dengan cara ini hingga akhirnya jatuh dari kudanya, mati. Teman-temannya meninggalkannya dan kembali ke kaumnya. Ketika mereka memasuki perkampungan mereka, orang-orang datang kepada Arbad dan bertanya apa yang terjadi. Ia berkata, "Tidak banyak. Muhammad mengajak kami menyembah sesuatu... Aku berharap aku bersamanya sekarang, sehingga aku bisa menembaknya dengan anak panah dan membunuhnya!" Subhanallah! Betapa beraninya! Beberapa hari setelah ia mengucapkan hal ini, ia pergi untuk menjual untanya. Allah menyambarnya dan untanya dengan petir, yang memusnahkan keduanya. Allah menurunkan wahyu tentang 'Amir dan Arbad, "Allah mengetahui apa yang dikandung oleh setiap betina, dan apa yang dikurangi dan ditambah dalam rahim. Dan segala sesuatu ada ukurannya di sisi-Nya. Yang Mengetahui semua yang gaib dan yang nyata; Yang Mahabesar, Mahatinggi. Sama saja di antara kalian orang yang merahasiakan ucapannya dan orang yang menampakkannya dengan terang-terangan, dan orang yang bersembunyi di malam hari dan yang berjalan terang-terangan di siang hari. Bagi manusia ada malaikat yang selalu mengikutinya bergiliran, di muka dan di belakangnya, yang menjaganya atas perintah Allah.
